original: Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows, the final book in the series by J.K. Rowling, is published worldwide, and 11 million copies sell in 24 hours
Marvel announces 10 new superhero films at Comic-Con, including Blade withMahershala Ali,Natalie Portmanas a female Thor, and the first Asian American superhero film, Shang-Chi
World's fastest train debuts, a Chinese state-owned maglev bullet train with speeds of 600 kilometers per hour (373 miles per hour) in Qingdao, China
World's strongest dog, 80-kg St. Bernard, pulls a 2,909-kg load 27 meters
2,000-year-old stone slab used to grind spices unearthed in Óc Eo, South Vietnam, is the earliest evidence of curry in Southeast Asia and still smells of nutmeg
Diane Guerrero, American actress and singer (Encanto), born in Passaic, New Jersey
Jean-Claude Droyer climbs Eiffel Tower in 2 hrs 18 mins
Soviet spacecraftSoyuz 19returns to Earth after the historic 'handshake in space' with an Apollo spacecraft
NASA's Space Shuttle program ends with the landing of Space Shuttle Atlantis on mission STS-135
World's lowest-ever natural temperature is recorded: -89.2°C (-128.6°F; 184.0 K) at the Soviet Vostok Station in Antarctica, surpassing record at same location set in 1960
Pleasure Island plans are unveiled as part of the Walt Disney World Village, now Disney Springs, in Florida
NASA launches Mercury 4 (Liberty Bell) with Gus Grissom
USSR launches Mars 4 for fly-by (2600 km) of red planet
Hottest day on Earth: average global temperature of 17.09°C (62.76°F) surpasses July 6, 2023, record according to EU's Copernicus Climate Change Service; record is broken the next day
UK records temperatures over 40°C (104°F) for the first time (record 40.3°C at Coningsby) amid an extreme heatwave across much of Europe
India scraps tax on sanitary products after campaign by activists

Pink Floyd's "The Wall" is performed where the Berlin Wall once stood in a charity concert led byRoger Waters, with guestsCyndi Lauper,Joni Mitchell, The Scorpions, Sinéad O'Connor,Bryan Adams, The Band,Van Morrison, The Hooters, and others
Detroit Tigers and Philadelphia A's play 24 inning 1-1 tie
The fully restored USS Constitution (aka "Old Ironsides") celebrates her 200th birthday, setting sail for the first time in 116 years
Isabell Werth, German equestrian, record for the most Olympian medals by an equestrian (12), born in Sevelen, West Germany
Gemini X returns to Earth
Russia's Luna 15 impacts moon after 52 lunar orbits
After 13 years, the 4,607-mile Trans-Siberian Railway is completed
Central Park in New York createdwhen New York State Legislature puts aside more than 750 acres of land on Manhattan Island
"Guardians of the Galaxy", directed by James Gunn, starringChris Prattand Zoe Saldana, premieres in Hollywood, Los Angeles
1st black to win a major US tennis tournament (Althea Gibson)
National Women's Hall of Fame, honoring American women is dedicated in Seneca Falls, NY
Marita Koch of East Germany sets world women's mark for 200m, 21.71s
Aswan High Dam opens in Egypt, enabling human control of the flooding of the Nile
Jesse Jamesand James Younger gang's 1st train robbery at Adair, Iowa
Tamika Catchings, American basketball player (Indiana Fever), born in Stratford, New Jersey
Jasmine Cephas Jones, British-American actress (Hamilton; #freerayshawn), born in London, England
113°F (45°C), near Gallipolis, Ohio (state record)
New Zealand beats Australia, 26-12 at Eden Park, Auckland to win their 3rd consecutive Tri Nations Rugby Series; flyhalfDan Carterlands 7 penalties for the All Blacks
Musical film "Stormy Weather," directed by Andrew L. Stone and starringBill Robinson, Lena Horne, andFats Waller(singing "Ain't Misbehavin'"), premieres in the US
Thunderstorm cuts shortDiana Ross's free concert in Central Park, New York City
Polly Matzinger, French-American immunologist (danger model for the immune system), born in La Seyne, France
Amar Singh Chamkila [Dhanni Ram], Punjabi folk singer "The Elvis of Punjab", born in Dugri, Punjab, India (d. 1988)
Eivør [Pálsdóttir], Faroese singer-songwriter, born in Syðrugøta, Faroe Islands
Louise Blanchard Bethune, 1st American woman architect, born in Waterloo, New York (d. 1915)

Milan Rastislav Štefánik, Slovak politician, French army general (WWI), Czecho-Slovak nationalist resistance leader, aviator, and astronomer, born in Kosaras, Kingdom of Hungary, Austria-Hungary (d. 1919)
MLB Cleveland Indians (9) and New York Yankees (7) combine to hit a record 16 doubles in 17-8 home team win at Dunn Field, Cleveland, Ohio
110°F (43°C) at Millsboro, Delaware (state record)
Last Dutch whaling ship Willem Barents Sea sold to Japan
US announces Lebanon freed American hostage David Dodge
"Sgt Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band" film starring TheBee Gees, based on the Beatles' album of the same name, premieres in NYC
Ferguson Jenkins,Gaylord Perry,Rod Carew,Tony Lazzeri, andBill Veeck, Jr are inducted into the Baseball Hall of Fame in Cooperstown, New York

With a record six races remaining, German Ferrari driverMichael Schumacherclinches his fifth F1 World Drivers' Championship with victory in the French Grand Prix at Circuit de Nevers Magny-Cours, his third straight title win
Isaac Stern, Ukrainian-American violinist (debut San Francisco Symphony), born in Kremenets, Ukraine, Soviet Union (d. 2001)
Don Knotts, American actor (Andy Griffth Show, 3's Company), born in Morgantown, West Virginia (d. 2006)
Plas Johnson, American be-bop jazz and session saxophonist (sax solo on "The Pink Panther Theme"), born in Donaldsonville, Louisiana
Viktor Mamatov, Russian biathlete (Olympic gold USSR 4 × 7.5k relay 1968, 72; World C'ship gold 20k individual 1967, 4 × 7.5k relay 1969, 70, 71), born in Belovo, Russia (d. 2023)
Jeff Fatt, Chinese-Australian actor (The Wiggles), born in Casino, Australia
Lee Aaron [Karen Lynn Greening], Canadian rock singer (Metal Queen), born in Belleville, Ontario
Paul Casey, English golfer (Ryder Cup wins 2004, 06, 18; 3 PGA Tour titles, 13 European Tour titles; PGA C'ship 2020 runner-up), born in Cheltenham, England
Yolanda Gampp, Canadian baker known for her YouTube channel "How to Cake It", born in Toronto, Ontario[1][2]
Damian Marley, Jamaican musician, born in Kingston, Jamaica
Kyoko Iwasaki, Japanese swimmer (Olympic gold 200m breaststroke 1992), born in Numazu, Japan
Juno Temple, English actress (Ted Lasso), born in London, England
Spanish explorer Álvaro de Mendaña is the first European to discover the Marquesas Island in Eastern Polynesia
